result.summary: Sinch exposes multiple authenticated developer surfaces including HTTP APIs (e.g. Numbers, Conversation, Subprojects, Access Keys Management) and a CLI, using either project access keys (Key ID + Key Secret) via Basic auth, OAuth 2.0 client-credentials bearer tokens derived from those keys, and for SMS/Voice some product-specific credentials like SMS API tokens and Voice app key/secret.

sinch_access_key.label: Sinch access key (Key ID + Key Secret for a project)
sinch_access_key.setup: In the [Sinch Build Dashboard](https://dashboard.sinch.com/settings/access-keys), open **Access keys** and click **Create Access Key**. Save the `Key ID`, `Key Secret`, and associated `project ID`; the `Key Secret` is only shown at creation time. These credentials can be used directly with HTTP Basic auth for testing, or exchanged at [`https://auth.sinch.com/oauth2/token`](https://auth.sinch.com/oauth2/token) using the `client_credentials` grant to obtain a short-lived bearer token.

sinch_oauth_access_token.label: Sinch OAuth 2.0 access token
sinch_oauth_access_token.setup: First create a project access key in the [Sinch Build Dashboard](https://dashboard.sinch.com/settings/access-keys). Then exchange the `Key ID` and `Key Secret` for a bearer token at [`https://auth.sinch.com/oauth2/token`](https://auth.sinch.com/oauth2/token) with `grant_type=client_credentials`. The returned `access_token` is typically valid for one hour and is sent as `Authorization: Bearer ...` on supported APIs.

sinch_sms_token.label: Sinch SMS API token
sinch_sms_token.setup: Open the [Sinch Build Dashboard SMS REST API page](https://dashboard.sinch.com/sms/api/rest). Your `API Token` and `Service Plan ID` are shown there; click **Show** to reveal the token. The token is used to authenticate SMS API calls, typically together with the `service_plan_id` in the request path or payload.

sinch_voice_app_key.label: Sinch Voice application key + secret
sinch_voice_app_key.setup: In the [Sinch Build Dashboard Voice apps page](https://dashboard.sinch.com/voice/apps), open your voice app (the default **Test Application** is created automatically for new accounts) and note the app `key` and `secret`. Assign a number to the app if needed. Use the app key as the HTTP Basic username and the app secret as the password for Voice API requests.

variables.name: projectId
variables.resolveFrom: Sinch Build Dashboard access keys page
variables.description: Project ID associated with the access key.

variables.name: parentProjectId
variables.resolveFrom: Sinch Build Dashboard access keys page
variables.description: Parent project ID used in the Subprojects API path.

variables.name: projectId
variables.description: Project ID used in Conversation API URLs.
